data.title: Gold seen climbing to $5,000, silver to sell off after hitting $100, BNP Paribas analyst says
data.description:
New geopolitical uncertainties including President Trump's new Greenland tariffs and concerns about the Federal Reserve's independence look primed to push gold prices to $5,000/oz sooner than expected, but the easing of physical dislocations should see silver sell off soon after reaching $100/oz, BNP Paribas Director of Commodities Strategy David Wilson said.

"Gold thrives on uncertainty," Wilson told Bloomberg on Monday. "We saw it through last year - we were obviously hitting highs all the way through last year - and continue to see that uncertainty at play now."

"Every type of support that you see for gold is at play at the moment, supporting the yellow metal," Wilson said. "We forecast back in November that we thought that gold was going to hit $5,000 sooner or later. It looked quite a big call back in November, [but] when you look at it now, we're at $4,700. $5,000 is not much of a stretch."

But silver is due for a correction, Wilson said, suggestiing that even as the metal continues to set new highs, the physical shortages that drove 2025's strong rally are resolving.

The physical market for silver is "definitely easing, lease rates are dropping back quite substantially, so those kinds of issues look like they're dissipating," and silver is a much thinner, less liquid market than gold, Wlson said. "As soon as some of the speculative flows start looking to take profits, then it's quite likely we could get quite a significant correction."

Gold and silver futures vaulted to new record highs Tuesday after President Trump ramped up his rhetoric about acquiring Greenland, with his threats of slapping tariffs on several European countries rekindling fears of a potential trade war.

Meanwhile, the U.S. dollar posted its largest daily decline in more than a month, making greenback-priced gold more affordable for overseas buyers.

President Trump's "latest escalation against NATO allies is likely to further shake market participants' confidence in the U.S. dollar as a safe haven and drive them to other safe havens," Commerzbank analyst Carsten Fritsch said in a note. "The ultimate safe haven is gold, which is largely immune to the influence of politicians and central banks."

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for January delivery closed +3.7% to $4,759.60/oz, the metal's largest one-day dollar gain on record and biggest one-day percentage increase since April 9, 2020, and front-month Comex January silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended +6.9% to $94.206/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Pathfinder Ventures announces $2M private placement
data.description:
• Pathfinder Ventures (RV:CA) plans a non-brokered private placement of 40M shares for gross proceeds of $2M.
  • Shares include half a warrant, with full warrants exercisable at $0.10 for 36 months.
  • Proceeds will fund working capital, RV resort developments, expansions, and acquisition op

data.title: Currenc Group receives Nasdaq notice over annual meeting compliance
data.description:
• Currenc Group (Nasdaq: CURR) on Tuesday said that it received a notice from Nasdaq that it is not in compliance with listing rules after failing to hold an annual shareholders’ meeting within 12 months of its fiscal year-end.
  • The Singapore-based fintech company said the notice, received on January 12, has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of its shares.
  • Currenc has 45 days to submit a plan to regain compliance.
  • The company said it plans to consult with Nasdaq on whether holding its annual meeting, scheduled for February 25, would restore compliance, and that Nasdaq could grant up to 180 days from the fiscal year-end to regain compliance.
  • CURR -2.66% after hours to $1.46.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sonoco COO Rodger Fuller announces retirement
data.description:
• Sonoco Products Company (NYSE: SON) announced on Tuesday that COO Rodger Fuller will retire effective February 28, 2026, concluding a 40-year career with the company.
  • Fuller has served as COO since 2020 and most recently acted as interim CEO of the company’s Metal Packaging EMEA business through the end of 2025.
  • Following his retirement, the company will not appoint a new COO; instead, the presidents of each business unit will report directly to President and CEO Howard Coker.
  • Shares -2.07%.

data.title: Fifth Third outlines $850M synergy target and 40%–45% adjusted revenue growth for 2026 through Comerica integration
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Timothy Spence, Chairman, CEO & President, opened by emphasizing the company's continued focus on stability, profitability, and growth: "Today, we reported earnings per share of $1.04 or $1.08, excluding certain items outlined on Page 2 of the release. We achieved an adjusted return on equity of 14.5% and adjusted return on assets of 1.41% and an adjusted efficiency ratio of 54.3%, all among the best of all banks regardless of size who have reported thus far."
  • Spence highlighted key product and regional achievements, including 5% year-over-year growth in average loans, 6% growth in net interest income, and 13% growth in wealth and asset management fees. He also noted the expansion in the Southeast region, with 50 new branches opened in 2025 and significant household growth in Georgia and the Carolinas.
  • The CEO announced, "We have received all material regulatory and shareholder approvals to complete our merger with Comerica. 99.7% of Fifth Third votes and 97% of Comerica votes cast were in favor of the merger."
  • Spence outlined four areas of revenue synergy focus post-merger: scaling Comerica's middle market platform, deepening commercial and wealth management relationships, building out retail banking in Texas with 150 de novo branches, and developing an Innovation Banking business.
  • Bryan Preston, EVP & CFO, stated: "Our results show what disciplined execution delivers in an uncertain environment, record full year NII of $6 billion and $9 billion in total revenue, improving asset quality and top quartile returns and efficiency."

Outlook

  • Preston announced, "We expect full year NII to range between $8.6 billion and $8.8 billion."
  • Full year adjusted noninterest income is expected to be between $4 billion and $4.4 billion.
  • "We expect full year noninterest expense to be between $7 billion and $7.3 billion, excluding the impact of anticipated CDI amortization and the $1.3 billion in estimated acquisition-related charges. This guidance assumes the realization of 37.5% of the $850 million of annualized run rate expense synergies in 2026," Preston noted.
  • Preston stated, "Our guide implies full year adjusted revenue and adjusted PPNR, excluding CDI amortization, to be up 40% to 45% over 2025 and another 100 to 200 basis points of positive operating leverage."
  • The company expects net charge-offs to range between 30 and 40 basis points in 2026, incorporating Comerica's loan portfolio.
  • Preston confirmed, "We currently expect CET1 capital post close of the Comerica acquisition to remain near our 10.5% target."

Financial Results

  • Spence reported, "Adjusted fourth quarter revenues rose 5% year-over-year, driven by 6% growth in net interest income, 8% growth in commercial payments fees and 13% growth in wealth and asset management fees."
  • Preston stated, "Net interest income was $1.5 billion for the quarter, a 6% increase over last year as net interest margin expanded 16 basis points, finishing the year at 3.13%."
  • Tangible book value per share grew 21% year-over-year, with CET1 ratio at 10.8%.
  • Preston reported, "Adjusted PPNR for the quarter was over $1 billion, a 6% increase from the prior year."
  • Preston highlighted, "Interest-bearing deposit costs were 2.28% in the fourth quarter, down 40 basis points year-over-year."
  • The company returned $1.6 billion of capital to shareholders in 2025.
  • Preston noted, "Net charge-offs were 40 basis points for the quarter, the lowest level in the past 7 quarters."

Q&A

  • Ebrahim Poonawala, BofA Securities: Asked about the biggest opportunities in the Comerica integration and what is most near term versus long term. Spence explained, "The most immediate near-term opportunity is going to come from some of the things we can do tactically in both leaning into Comerica's existing customer base as well as what our deposit marketing, analytically driven deposit marketing and product strategies will allow us to do in Comerica's branch network, followed by... the build-out of the Texas markets from a retail distribution perspective... the ramp-up of the Innovation Banking business."
  • Gerard Cassidy, RBC: Asked about integration progress and customer conversion timeline. Spence stated, "We're going to move the conversion up to Labor Day from what would have otherwise been a mid-October time frame."
  • Robert Siefers, Piper Sandler: Asked about rate sensitivity post-close and balance sheet evolution. Preston responded, "We're always targeting to be relatively rate neutral, especially in a normal environment... the natural balance sheet becomes a lot more asset sensitive given the merger... We'll probably still be a little bit asset sensitive when all is said and done, but we'll be in a good manageable position."
  • John Pancari, Evercore ISI: Inquired about changes to Comerica transaction assumptions. Preston replied, "No material changes to any of the assumptions inherent in the transaction."
  • Michael Mayo, Wells Fargo: Asked about achieving 2027 targets earlier. Spence confirmed, "We are expecting -- we talked about 9% EPS accretion in 2027. We would expect to be able to deliver 9% EPS accretion from the deal in the fourth quarter of 2026."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was probing and focused on the execution of the Comerica merger, integration timelines, and specific synergy realization, with a generally positive view of the company’s preparedness and opportunity set.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone, especially when discussing merger integration and future financial targets, as seen in Spence’s statement: "I'm even more confident in our ability to realize the benefits of the combination."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' sentiment shifted from cautious to more optimistic about integration, while management’s confidence increased, with more specific targets and earlier timelines presented.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 now includes specific targets for NII ($8.6 billion–$8.8 billion), adjusted noninterest income ($4 billion–$4.4 billion), and noninterest expense ($7 billion–$7.3 billion), compared to a more general positive outlook previously.
  • Management advanced the timeline for Comerica integration, moving systems conversion up to Labor Day from mid-October.
  • Analysts focused more on integration logistics and synergy realization than in the prior quarter, reflecting the close proximity to merger completion.
  • Management’s tone became more confident, with Spence stating the 2027 targets could be achieved by Q4 2026.
  • Financial performance showed improved NIM, efficiency ratio, and tangible book value growth over the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Integration of Comerica presents execution risk, especially around achieving projected synergies and timeline targets.
  • Management referenced the need to manage balance sheet mix and funding costs during the transition.
  • Preston noted, "We do not expect material onetime charges related to these actions," but acknowledged $1.3 billion in estimated acquisition-related charges.
  • Credit normalization and potential impacts from incorporating Comerica’s loan portfolio are anticipated, with guidance for net charge-offs between 30 and 40 basis points.

Final Takeaway

Fifth Third’s management emphasized the accelerated integration of Comerica, with all key approvals secured and a focus on achieving $850 million in expense synergies. The company projects substantial adjusted revenue and PPNR growth of 40% to 45% in 2026 and targets achieving 2027 profitability and efficiency metrics by the fourth quarter of 2026, highlighting strong momentum in core business performance and confidence in post-merger execution and value creation for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: LightPath Technologies acquires Amorphous Materials for $7M
data.description:
• LightPath Technologies (NASDAQ:LPTH) said on Tuesday it has acquired the assets of Amorphous Materials for $7M in cash, with the deal also including up to $3M in additional equity consideration tied to technical milestones.
  • The acquisition advances LightPath’s strategy to become a vertically integrated infrared imaging solutions provider in the $9 billion global infrared imaging market.
  • It expands large-diameter optics capabilities, increasing BlackDiamond™ glass production capacity to up to 17 inches from 5 inches, enabling the supply of large, high-value optics for long-range satellite sensors, including Golden Dome applications.
  • Shares +11.38%.

data.title: WSP Global prices $1B senior notes offering
data.description:
• WSP Global (WSP:CA) priced a $1B offering of senior unsecured notes split between 2032 and 2036 maturities.
  • The notes carry fixed interest rates of 4.003% and 4.586%, respectively, with the offering expected to close in late January 2026.
  • Proceeds will help fund WSP’s pending acquisition of TRC Companies or, if the deal does not close, repay existing debt.

data.title: SmartFinancial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.81 beats by $0.03, revenue of $53.31M beats by $1.92M
data.description:
• SmartFinancial press release (SMBK): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.81 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $53.31M (+13.9% Y/Y) beats by $1.92M.

data.title: KKR's Americas buyout fund on track to beat $20B target - report
data.description:
KKR (KKR) expects to exceed the $20B target for its flagship Americas private equity fund, setting a record for the strategy, according to a media report on Tuesday.

The alternative asset manager may hit the $22B hard cap for the North America Fund XIV as it expects to close in Q1 after ~18 months of fundraising, Bloomberg News reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The firm exceeded the $19B raised for the previous fund, a development that underscores the strength of KKR's (KKR) private equity business. 

The company started fundraising for the fund in June 2024 and is on pace to hit the hard cap amid a challenging environment where fundraising and asset sales are challenged by elevated interest rates and economic uncertainty. 

KKR (KKR) stock ticked up 0.1% in after-hours trading.
